contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: DApp: Facebook-style notification badge for action items (v1)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/233
  
  Details:
  Shipped a shared red-badge notification widget across all 33 DApp pages so the operator can spot pending action items at a glance.
  
  ## What shipped (dapp PR #233, squash-merged)
  - js/notifications.js (new) — fixed-position bell + red counter top-right corner, click-to-open popup with per-module breakdown
  - menu.js — async-injects notifications.js after rendering the nav menu (one upstream change covers all 33 pages)
  - 33 HTML pages — menu.js cache-busted to ?v=20260512a (32 were stale on 20260510)
  - service-worker.js — notifications.js added to URLS_TO_CACHE
  
  ## Module contract
  Each module is a source registered via Notifications.register({id, fetch}). fetch() returns null (silently hidden) or {count, label, sublabel, link, items}. Sources fire in parallel; failures are skipped gracefully. New modules plug in without touching the widget.
  
  ## Modules wired in v1
  1. Outbound Review — sums the three warmup-review cohorts (AI/Warm-up + AI/Follow-up + AI/Prospect Replied) from the existing getWarmupReviewQueue endpoint. Zero new GAS code needed.
  2. Partner Check-in follow-ups due — registered with a placeholder fetch against a proposed GAS action get_partner_followups_due on tdg_shipping_planner. Until that action is deployed (manual clasp-push pending per project_partner_check_in memory), the source returns null and is silently hidden. Cadence rule proposed: no check-in in last 30 days for a Partner-status store.
  
  ## Anti-pattern guarded
  Per the new feedback_check_tracking_before_recommending_action memory, the badge intentionally surfaces 'where the operator's review is the bottleneck' rather than raw queue depth. The warmup module sums the three pre-categorized cohorts (which represent the gate output, not the raw queue of 67). If the badge over-pressures in practice, the next iteration adds a 'send-ready' filter at the GAS layer.
  
  ## v1.1 follow-up (separate PR, requires GAS clasp-push)
  - Add get_partner_followups_due action to tdg_shipping_planner Apps Script
  - Optional: ?send_ready=true filter on getWarmupReviewQueue
  
  PR: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/233 (merged as commit dcf2d08)
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
